description_long: This is a small, single-story brick house with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in reddish-brown shingles. The front facade features two teal-colored metal doors, each with warning signage, and a smaller teal utility door with a vent, suggesting a possible utility or service function. The brickwork is uniform and dark red, with black trim outlining the roof edges and a prominent black downspout running vertically along the front and side. The roof is simple and unadorned, with no visible windows or decorative elements, and the side and rear walls are solid brick with no openings. The utilitarian design and signage indicate this building may serve as a utility substation or service structure in an urban environment.
description: A small, single-story brick house with a steep gable roof, teal metal doors, and prominent warning signage. The design is utilitarian, with no windows and black roof trim and downspouts.
description_view_0: Front view showing two teal metal doors with warning signs and a smaller utility door with a vent, all set in a brick wall with a black downspout.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the reddish-brown shingled gable roof and a solid brick wall with no openings.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a plain brick wall with no doors or windows, and the gable roof above.
description_view_3: Angled view highlighting the two main teal doors with signage, the utility door, and the black downspout along the brick facade.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway consists of two teal metal doors with warning signage, flush with the brick facade, and a smaller utility door with a vent; no porch, steps, or decorative elements.
